info.title: Atom Registrar API
info.description: REST API for domain registration, DNS management, and domain operations. Authentication via Bearer token in Authorization header.

get.description: Get a paginated list of all domains owned by the authenticated user. Supports search, filtering by extension, and pagination.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Number of results per page (default: 20, max: 100)

get.description: Check if a single domain name is available for registration. Some results may be cached.

post.description: Check availability for multiple domain names (max 50 domains per request). Some results may be cached.
Register a new domain name. Performs a real-time availability check, processes payment (account balance or saved card), and registers the domain with a 60-day transfer lock and parking nameservers.

Initiate a domain transfer-in to Atom Registrar. Validates eligibility (domain not already owned, no transfer in progress, correct auth code, no transfer lock, not registered within 60 days), resolves pricing, processes payment via account balance or saved card, then initiates the transfer with the upstream registrar. A confirmation email is sent when the transfer is accepted.

409.description: Domain already in Atom Registrar or transfer already in progress

422.description: Transfer not eligible (locked, invalid authcode, status, or 60-day restriction)

post.description: Initiate transfer-in for up to 10 domains in a single request. All domains are validated and priced first; a single payment is charged for the aggregate total. Transfers are then initiated one by one. Domains that fail to transfer are individually refunded to your account balance (if paid via balance) or flagged for manual review (if paid via card).

201.description: Bulk transfer complete (may include partial failures)

402.description: Payment failed for the full batch

get.description: Get detailed information about a specific domain including status, expiry date, nameservers, DNSSEC status, and contact information.
Get all active DNS records for a domain. Syncs with PowerDNS before returning results.

post.description: Create a new DNS record for the domain. Supports A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT and other standard record types.

put.description: Update an existing DNS record. All fields are optional; only provided fields will be updated.

delete.description: Delete a specific DNS record by its ID.

get.description: Get the current nameservers configured for a domain. Returns nameserver list and type (atom, parking, or custom).

put.description: Update nameservers for a domain. Supports atom, parking, or custom nameservers. Automatically disables DNSSEC if switching nameserver types.
Enable or disable WHOIS privacy protection for a domain. May require contact verification via email.

202.description: Privacy protection update accepted, verification required

put.description: Enable or disable automatic renewal for a domain before expiration.

Assign contact handles to one or more roles on a domain — `registrant`, `admin`, `billing`, and/or `tech`. At least one role must be supplied; roles that are omitted remain unchanged.

If the registrar requires email verification (e.g. registrant change), a **202** is returned and the update is applied once the verification email is approved.

The domain must have `domain_contact_management` enabled (active, non-locked domain with Atom nameservers).

202.description: Accepted — verification email sent. The update is applied once the user approves via email.

message.example: Domain contact update initiated. Please verify the update via the email sent to your registered email address.

get.description: Get all active URL forwarding rules configured for a domain.

post.description: Create a new URL forwarding rule for a domain. Supports permanent (301) and temporary (302) redirects.

delete.description: Delete a specific URL forwarding rule by its ID.

get.description: Filter DNS records by subdomain and/or record type. Useful for finding specific records.

parameters.name: subdomain
parameters.description: Subdomain to filter by (use '@' for root domain)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: DNS record type to filter by (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, etc.)

put.description: Update all DNS records matching specific subdomain and type criteria. Updates content and optionally TTL for all matching records.

delete.description: Delete all DNS records matching specific subdomain and type criteria. Type parameter is required.

parameters.name: subdomain
parameters.description: Subdomain to filter by (use '@' for root domain, optional)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: DNS record type to delete (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, etc.)

get.description: Get DNSSEC DS records for a domain. Returns parsed records with algorithm and digest type names.

post.description:
Enable DNSSEC for a domain.

## Nameserver behavior

| Nameserver type | Key fields (`flags`, `algorithm`, `protocol`, `public_key`) |
|---|---|
| **Atom / Parking nameservers** | **Ignored.** DNSSEC is fully auto-managed: Atom generates and rotates keys automatically. You do not need to supply any key fields — if you do, they are silently discarded. |
| **Custom nameservers** | **Required.** All four fields must be provided. The supplied key is pushed directly to the upstream registrar. |

> **Note:** If your domain uses Atom or Parking nameservers (e.g. `ns1.atom.com`, `ns1.squadhelp.com`, `ns1.atomregistrar.com`), the algorithm, protocol, flags, and public key in the request body have **no effect**. DNSSEC is automatically applied and managed by Atom.
Disable DNSSEC for a domain. Required before changing nameservers.

get.description: Returns all contact handles associated with the authenticated user — the primary registrant contact (RegistrantUser) plus any additional contact handles.

post.description: Creates a new registrar contact handle. If no primary registrant contact (RegistrantUser) exists for the account, this request creates one. Subsequent calls create additional contact handles. The contact is also registered with the upstream registrar (RealtimeRegister).

409.description: Conflict — a contact with the same label already exists

422.description: Registrar rejected the contact — see `violations` for field-level errors

put.description: Updates an existing contact handle. Only the fields provided in the request body are changed — omitted fields keep their current values. If the registrar requires email verification for the change (e.g. email address updates), a 202 response is returned and the update is applied once the user approves the verification email.

parameters.name: handle
parameters.description: The registrar contact handle to update (e.g. `ATOM-12345` or `CONTACT_1-67890`)

schema.description: All fields are optional — only provide the fields you want to change.

202.description: Accepted — verification email sent. The contact will be updated once the user approves via email.

message.example: Verification email sent. Please approve the contact update via email to complete the change.

409.description: Conflict — label already used by another contact

422.description: Registrar rejected the update — see `violations` for field-level errors

BearerAuth.description: Registrar API Bearer token. Obtain from dashboard at /dashboard/seller/api-access after requesting Registrar API access.

error.description: Error message if status is error or invalid

UpdateDomainContactsRequest.description: At least one of `registrant`, `admin`, `billing`, or `tech` must be provided. Omitted roles are left unchanged.

registrant.description: Contact handle to set as the registrant. Must belong to the authenticated user.

admin.description: Contact handle to set as the administrative contact.

billing.description: Contact handle to set as the billing contact.

tech.description: Contact handle to set as the technical contact.

EnableDnssecRequest.description:
DNSSEC key fields for enabling DNSSEC.

**Atom / Parking nameservers:** All fields are optional and will be ignored — DNSSEC is auto-managed.

**Custom nameservers:** `flags`, `algorithm`, `protocol`, and `public_key` are required. `digest_type` is optional (defaults to `2` / SHA-256).

flags.description: DNSKEY flags. `256` = ZSK (Zone Signing Key), `257` = KSK (Key Signing Key). **Ignored for Atom/Parking nameservers.**

algorithm.description:
DNSKEY algorithm number. **Ignored for Atom/Parking nameservers.**

| Value | Algorithm |
|---|---|
| `3` | DSA/SHA-1 |
| `5` | RSA/SHA-1 |
| `6` | DSA-NSEC3-SHA1 |
| `7` | RSASHA1-NSEC3-SHA1 |
| `8` | RSA/SHA-256 |
| `10` | RSA/SHA-512 |
| `12` | GOST R 34.10-2001 |
| `13` | ECDSA P-256/SHA-256 |
| `14` | ECDSA P-384/SHA-384 |
| `15` | Ed25519 |
| `16` | Ed448 |
| `17` | SM2/SM3 |
| `23` | GOST R 34.10-2012 |

protocol.description: DNSKEY protocol. Must be `3` (the only valid value per RFC 4034). **Ignored for Atom/Parking nameservers.**

public_key.description: Base64-encoded public key material from your DNS provider. **Ignored for Atom/Parking nameservers.**

digest_type.description:
Digest algorithm used to compute the DS record returned in the response. Defaults to `2` (SHA-256). **Ignored for Atom/Parking nameservers.**

| Value | Algorithm | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `1` | SHA-1 | Legacy; avoid for new deployments |
| `2` | SHA-256 | **Recommended** (default) |
| `3` | GOST R 34.11-94 | Requires server-side GOST engine |
| `4` | SHA-384 | High-security deployments |

auto_managed.description: `true` when Atom/Parking nameservers are in use and DNSSEC is auto-managed. `false` when custom nameservers are used and caller-supplied keys were applied.

key_data.description: Active DNSKEY records. Populated for Atom/Parking NS; echoes supplied key for custom NS.

ds_record.description: DS record computed from the supplied DNSKEY using the requested `digest_type`. `null` for Atom/Parking nameservers (auto-managed) or if DS generation fails.

algorithm.description: DNSKEY algorithm number (matches the `algorithm` supplied in the request).

digestType.description: Digest type used (matches the `digest_type` supplied in the request, or `2` if omitted).

domain_name.description: The domain name to transfer in

authcode.description: Auth/EPP code from the current registrar. Required to authorize the transfer.

domains.description: List of domains to transfer (max 10 per request)

total_refunded.description: Amount refunded to account balance for failed transfers (only when payment method is account_balance)

error.description: Present only when status is failed

price_breakdown.description: Present only when status is Transfer Initiated

name.description: Subdomain name (use empty string or '@' for root domain)

type.description: Nameserver type: 'atom' uses Atom nameservers, 'parking' uses parking nameservers, 'custom' requires nameservers array

nameservers.description: Required if type is 'custom'. Array of nameserver hostnames.

enabled.description: Enable or disable WHOIS privacy protection

message.example: Please verify your contact info via email to complete privacy protection update

subdomain.description: Subdomain to update (use '@' for root domain, omit to update all subdomains)

content.description: New content value for all matching records

ttl.description: Optional: new TTL value for all matching records

type.description: Whether this is the primary registrant contact or an additional handle

label.description: Optional human-readable label for the contact

address.description: Street address as a string or array of lines.

label.description: Optional unique label to identify this contact

UnauthorizedError.description: Authentication failed - invalid or revoked API token

message.example: Invalid registrar API token or access has been revoked. Please request registrar API access from your dashboard.

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - operation not allowed for this domain
